title: Smoky Dutch Stew
description: A hearty stew made with smoky sausage, tender vegetables, and a hint of rosemary. This dish is perfect for a cold winter's day.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the smoky sausage and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Remove the sausage from the pot and set aside.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped carrot and potato to the pot and cook for 5 minutes.
Add the Dutch stew meat to the pot and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the beef broth, water, paprika, rosemary, salt, and black pepper to the pot.
Return the sausage to the pot and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 1 1/2 hours, or until the meat is tender.
Remove the bay leaves and serve hot, garnished with fresh rosemary if desired.
Serve with crusty bread or over mashed potatoes.
